report_name: American Lending Center Breach Exposes 123K SSNs from 2025 Ransomware Attack

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Threat Actor
threat_actors.description: An unidentified threat actor conducted a double extortion ransomware attack against the American Lending Center between July 24 and July 30, 2025. The actor encrypted files and exfiltrated sensitive data, including Social Security numbers and financial details, for over 123,000 individuals.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor successfully deployed ransomware to encrypt files on the American Lending Center's network to disrupt business operations.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor exfiltrated a significant amount of sensitive data, including PII and financial details, to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

identities.name: American Lending Center
identities.description: A California-based private lender that was the victim of a ransomware attack in July 2025, resulting in a data breach affecting 123,158 individuals.

infrastructure.name: Attacker Exfiltration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Unspecified attacker-controlled infrastructure used to exfiltrate sensitive data from the American Lending Center during the ransomware attack.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1c9e-5d7f-4b3a-8e9c-0f1d2e3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/blog/information-security/american-lending-center-data-breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though rare for shadow copy deletion in this manner.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware tactic suggested by hunting hints in the article.
